description: To provide for the repayment of the educational loan debt of qualified health professionals involved in minority health and health disparities research, for the purposes of improving minority health and reducing health disparities. Qualified health professionals who contractually agree to conduct qualified minority health disparities research or other health disparities research eligible to apply for this program.

grant_sections.definition: A concise summary of the proposed research project.
grant_sections.generation_instructions: Generate a concise, self-contained summary of the entire proposed project, suitable for public release via NIH RePORTER. The summary must clearly state the central hypothesis, the specific aims, the overall methodology, and the expected results. Maintain a professional, objective tone. Ensure the text is accessible yet scientifically accurate. Strictly adhere to NIH formatting and character limits (typically 30 lines or 4000 characters). Avoid including any Personal Identifiable Information (PII) or Protected Health Information (PHI). This section must function as a standalone description of the research scope.
guidelines: The Project Summary/Abstract will be made available to the public through the NIH Research Portfolio Online Reporting Tool (RePORTER).
Do not include sensitive Personal Identifiable Information (PII) or Protected Health Information (PHI) in this section.
search_queries: NIH Project Summary character limit
Example NIH R01 abstract structure
RePORTER project summary guidelines
Writing specific aims for NIH grants
grant_sections.title: Project Summary/Abstract

grant_sections.definition: A brief explanation of the relevance of the proposed research to public health.
grant_sections.generation_instructions: Draft a brief, compelling statement explaining the relevance of the proposed research to public health, suitable for public release via RePORTER. This statement must be written in plain language, understandable to a lay audience, while accurately reflecting the scientific goals. Clearly articulate how the project's findings will contribute to the prevention, diagnosis, treatment, or cure of human diseases, or how they will advance health equity. Do not repeat the abstract; focus solely on the broader societal implications and health outcomes. Avoid PII/PHI.
guidelines: The statement will be made available to the public through the NIH Research Portfolio Online Reporting Tool (RePORTER).
Do not include sensitive Personal Identifiable Information (PII) or Protected Health Information (PHI) in this section.
search_queries: NIH Public Health Relevance statement examples
Translational research impact metrics
Writing for a lay audience NIH grants
Linking basic science to public health outcomes
grant_sections.title: Public Health Relevance Statement

grant_sections.definition: The main detailed description of the proposed research, methodology, and scientific rationale.
grant_sections.generation_instructions: Develop the comprehensive Research Plan following the NIH structure: Specific Aims, Significance, Innovation, and Approach. The Approach section must be highly detailed, covering experimental design, methods, data analysis, and plans to address potential pitfalls and alternative strategies. Emphasize the scientific premise and preliminary data supporting feasibility. Explicitly address the criteria for Rigor and Reproducibility, including blinding, randomization, and sample size justification. Maintain a formal, evidence-based, and persuasive scientific tone. Ensure strict adherence to the page limits specified in the Funding Opportunity Announcement (FOA).
guidelines: Follow the standard instruction for the PHS 398 Research Plan Form (G.400), as well as any additional program-specific instructions.
Ensure all attachments adhere to formatting requirements for PDF files, fonts, margins, headers, and footers.
Remember that the Funding Opportunity Announcement (FOA) instructions always supersede these general application instructions.
search_queries: NIH R01 Specific Aims examples
Guidelines for Rigor and Reproducibility NIH
Statistical power calculation for preclinical studies
Preliminary data requirements for NIH R series
Addressing pitfalls and alternative strategies in research plan
PHS 398 Research Plan structure G.400
Best practices for experimental design justification
grant_sections.title: Research Plan

grant_sections.definition: A required narrative plan detailing how scientific data will be managed, preserved, and shared.
grant_sections.generation_instructions: Construct a detailed narrative addressing the eight required elements of the NIH Data Management and Sharing (DMS) Policy. Specify the types of scientific data to be generated, the standards (metadata) that will be applied, and the designated repository (e.g., dbGaP, GEO, institutional repository). Define the timeline for data sharing and the procedures for access and preservation. Clearly state who is responsible for DMS activities and justify the associated costs within the budget. The tone must be compliant and technically precise.
guidelines: Detail the strategies for managing and sharing scientific data generated during the project period.
Ensure the plan aligns with NIH policy regarding data management and sharing requirements.
search_queries: NIH Data Management and Sharing Policy required elements
Selecting appropriate NIH data repository
Budgeting for data curation and sharing costs
FAIR principles implementation in biomedical research
grant_sections.title: Data Management and Sharing (DMS) Plan

grant_sections.definition: Narrative detailing the involvement of human subjects and the design/protocol of any proposed clinical trials.
grant_sections.generation_instructions: Complete the PHS Human Subjects and Clinical Trials Information section (G.500). If a clinical trial is proposed, provide the full protocol details, including specific aims, background, study design (e.g., randomization, blinding), outcome measures, and statistical analysis plan. For all human subjects research, detail the protection of human subjects, including risk/benefit ratio, recruitment methods, and informed consent procedures. Ensure strict regulatory compliance language (e.g., IRB, IND/IDE status). Absolutely exclude Protected Health Information (PHI) from the narrative.
guidelines: Complete the PHS Human Subjects and Clinical Trials Information section (G.500).
Provide detailed protocols and justification if human subjects or clinical trials are involved.
Must not include Protected Health Information (PHI) in the application documents.
search_queries: PHS Human Subjects and Clinical Trials Information G.500
NIH definition of a clinical trial
Data Safety Monitoring Plan requirements NIH
Justification for inclusion/exclusion criteria in clinical trials
IRB approval process NIH grants
grant_sections.title: Human Subjects and Clinical Trials Narrative

grant_sections.definition: A narrative justification explaining the necessity and calculation of all requested budget items.
grant_sections.generation_instructions: Provide a clear, itemized justification for all requested funds. For personnel, specify the role, percentage of effort, and convert this to person-months, justifying the necessity of their time commitment relative to the Specific Aims. For non-personnel costs (supplies, travel, equipment), detail the items and explain why they are essential for the project's success. If a modular budget is used, justify the modules and any exceptions. If a detailed budget is required, ensure every line item is explained. The tone must be factual and fiscally responsible.
guidelines: Develop the budget justification based on the kinds of costs allowable (G.300/G.320 context).
Justify costs clearly, distinguishing between modular and detailed budget requirements.
Provide sufficient detail to allow reviewers to evaluate the necessity and appropriateness of the requested funds.
search_queries: Converting FTE to person months NIH
NIH modular budget justification guidelines
Allowable costs for NIH research grants
Justifying equipment costs in grant budget
grant_sections.title: Budget Justification

grant_sections.definition: Supplemental narrative required for Career Development (K) awards, focusing on the applicant's career goals and training environment.
grant_sections.generation_instructions: Generate the supplemental narrative required for Career Development (K) awards. Detail the candidate's career goals, demonstrating a clear path toward research independence. Describe the specific training activities (didactic, experiential) planned. Provide a robust description of the mentorship team, including their qualifications and the frequency/nature of interaction. The institutional commitment to the candidate's protected time and resources must be explicitly stated. Follow instructions specific to PHS 398 Career Development Award Supplemental Form (G.410).
guidelines: Follow instructions specific to the PHS 398 Career Development Award Supplemental Form (G.410).
Consult the G.130 Program Overview section for context regarding program-specific instructions.
Provide a detailed plan for career development, mentorship, and training.
search_queries: NIH K award career development plan examples
Justifying protected time for K award
Mentor statement requirements K award
G.410 PHS 398 Career Development Award instructions
grant_sections.title: Career Development Award Supplemental Narrative

grant_sections.definition: Supplemental narrative required for Research Training (T) programs, detailing the training environment and curriculum.
grant_sections.generation_instructions: Develop the comprehensive Research Training Program Plan (T Award supplement). Describe the program's structure, objectives, and the quality of the training faculty. Detail the recruitment and selection process for trainees, emphasizing efforts to enhance diversity. Provide a thorough description of the didactic and research curriculum, including specific modules for Responsible Conduct of Research (RCR) and professional skills development. Outline the mechanisms for program evaluation and tracking trainee outcomes. Follow instructions specific to PHS 398 Research Training Program Plan Form (G.420).
guidelines: Follow instructions specific to the PHS 398 Research Training Program Plan Form (G.420).
Consult the G.130 Program Overview section for context regarding program-specific instructions.
Describe the structure, curriculum, and faculty involved in the research training program.
search_queries: NIH T32 program plan examples
Responsible Conduct of Research training requirements NIH
Trainee selection and diversity plan T awards
G.420 PHS 398 Research Training Program Plan instructions
grant_sections.title: Research Training Program Plan Narrative

grant_sections.definition: Supplemental narrative required for Fellowship (F) applications, detailing the research and training plan.
grant_sections.generation_instructions: Prepare the supplemental narrative for Fellowship (F) applications. Focus on the applicant's potential and the quality of the proposed research training experience. Summarize the research project and detail the specific training goals that will be achieved under the fellowship. Clearly define the role of the sponsor/mentor, including their expertise and commitment to the applicant's development. Ensure the narrative demonstrates that the institutional environment is conducive to the proposed training. Follow instructions specific to PHS Fellowship Supplemental Form (G.430).
guidelines: Follow instructions specific to the PHS Fellowship Supplemental Form (G.430).
Consult the G.130 Program Overview section for context regarding program-specific instructions.
Detail the proposed research training experience and the role of the sponsor/mentor.
search_queries: NIH F32 research training plan guidelines
Sponsor statement requirements NIH fellowship
G.430 PHS Fellowship Supplemental Form instructions
Writing the research proposal for an NIH F award
grant_sections.title: Fellowship Supplemental Narrative

grant_sections.definition: A compliance narrative detailing how research resources generated by the project will be made available to the scientific community.
grant_sections.generation_instructions: Describe the plan for sharing research resources generated by the project (e.g., unique reagents, cell lines, animal models, software, or specialized data tools). Specify which resources will be shared and the mechanism (e.g., institutional core, public repository, commercial vendor). Detail the timeline for availability and any associated costs or restrictions (e.g., Material Transfer Agreements). Ensure the plan complies fully with NIH policies regarding the timely and broad dissemination of research resources.
guidelines: Describe the plan for sharing research resources, such as model organisms, reagents, or software.
Ensure the plan complies with NIH policies regarding the sharing of research resources.
search_queries: NIH Research Resource Sharing Plan guidelines
Sharing model organisms NIH policy
Material Transfer Agreement requirements for reagents
Software sharing best practices NIH grants
grant_sections.title: Resource Sharing Plan(s)

grant_sections.definition: A narrative description of the facilities, laboratory space, and institutional resources available for the project.
grant_sections.generation_instructions: Provide a narrative description of the institutional environment and facilities. Detail the laboratory space, animal facilities, and specialized core facilities (e.g., genomics, imaging, computing) available to the project team. Explain how the scientific environment is conducive to the success of the proposed research and fosters collaboration. Focus on the *relevance* and *adequacy* of the resources, avoiding generic institutional boilerplate. Ensure the description aligns with the Project/Performance Site Location(s) Form (G.230).
guidelines: Describe the institutional environment where the research will be conducted.
Detail the facilities, resources, and services available to the project team.
Explain how the scientific environment contributes to the probability of success.
search_queries: Writing Facilities and Other Resources section NIH
Describing core facility access in grant application
Institutional environment description examples
Computational resources justification NIH
grant_sections.title: Facilities and Other Resources

grant_sections.definition: A description of major equipment items necessary for the project.
grant_sections.generation_instructions: List and describe all major items of equipment (typically defined as costing over $5,000) that are essential for the proposed research. For each item, specify its location and confirm its availability and accessibility to the project team. If the equipment is shared, briefly describe the scheduling or access mechanism to demonstrate that the project will not be hindered by limited availability. Do not list equipment requested in the budget unless it is already owned and available.
guidelines: List and describe major items of equipment that are available for use in the proposed research.
If equipment is shared, specify the location and extent of availability to the project.
search_queries: NIH major equipment definition
Equipment list format for grant application
Justifying shared equipment access
grant_sections.title: Equipment

name: Loan Repayment Program for Health Disparities Research
